content:
First, place coordinates so that the line \(A\) is the \(x\!-\)axis and the big circle \(B\) has center \((0,R)\) with \(R=2008\), so that it is tangent to the \(x\)-axis at \(P=(0,0)\).  A circle \(C_n\) of radius \(r_n\) sits “below” \(B\) and above the \(x\)-axis, tangent to both, and to the previous circle \(C_{n-1}\).

1.  Tangency to the \(x\)-axis forces the center of \(C_n\) to be \((x_n,r_n)\).  
2.  External tangency to the big circle gives
   \[
     \sqrt{x_n^2+(r_n-R)^2}\;=\;R+r_n
     \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
     x_n^2=4R\,r_n,
     \quad\text{so}\quad
     x_n=2\sqrt{R\,r_n}\,. 
   \]
3.  External tangency to \(C_{n-1}\) of radius \(r_{n-1}\) with center \((x_{n-1},r_{n-1})\) gives
   \[
     \sqrt{(x_n-x_{n-1})^2+(r_n-r_{n-1})^2}
     =r_n+r_{n-1}
     \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
     x_n-x_{n-1}=2\sqrt{\,r_n\,r_{n-1}\,}\,. 
   \]
4.  Hence
   \[
     2\sqrt{R\,r_n}
     =2\sqrt{R\,r_{n-1}}+2\sqrt{r_n\,r_{n-1}}
     \;\Longrightarrow\;
     \sqrt{r_n}=\frac{\sqrt R\,\sqrt{r_{n-1}}}{\sqrt R-\sqrt{r_{n-1}}}\,.
   \]
   If we set \(s_n=\sqrt{r_n}\) and \(c=\sqrt R\), this recurrence is
   \[
     s_n=\frac{c\,s_{n-1}}{\,c-s_{n-1}\,}, 
     \quad s_1=1.
   \]
   One checks by induction that
   \[
     s_n=\frac{c}{\,c-(n-1)\,}\,,
     \]
   so
   \[
     r_n=s_n^2
        =\frac{R}{\bigl(c-(n-1)\bigr)^2}
        =\frac{2008}{\bigl(\sqrt{2008}-(n-1)\bigr)^2}\,.
   \]

5.  In order for \(r_n\) to remain positive (so the circle is geometrically meaningful), we need
   \[
     \sqrt{2008}-(n-1)>0
     \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
     n-1<\sqrt{2008}\approx44.82
     \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
     n<45.82.
   \]
   Hence the largest integer \(n\) is 
   \[
     n_{\max}=45.
   \]

Answer: \(\boxed{45}\).

message.content:
First, place coordinates so that the line \(A\) is the \(x\!-\)axis and the big circle \(B\) has center \((0,R)\) with \(R=2008\), so that it is tangent to the \(x\)-axis at \(P=(0,0)\).  A circle \(C_n\) of radius \(r_n\) sits “below” \(B\) and above the \(x\)-axis, tangent to both, and to the previous circle \(C_{n-1}\).

1.  Tangency to the \(x\)-axis forces the center of \(C_n\) to be \((x_n,r_n)\).  
2.  External tangency to the big circle gives
   \[
     \sqrt{x_n^2+(r_n-R)^2}\;=\;R+r_n
     \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
     x_n^2=4R\,r_n,
     \quad\text{so}\quad
     x_n=2\sqrt{R\,r_n}\,. 
   \]
3.  External tangency to \(C_{n-1}\) of radius \(r_{n-1}\) with center \((x_{n-1},r_{n-1})\) gives
   \[
     \sqrt{(x_n-x_{n-1})^2+(r_n-r_{n-1})^2}
     =r_n+r_{n-1}
     \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
     x_n-x_{n-1}=2\sqrt{\,r_n\,r_{n-1}\,}\,. 
   \]
4.  Hence
   \[
     2\sqrt{R\,r_n}
     =2\sqrt{R\,r_{n-1}}+2\sqrt{r_n\,r_{n-1}}
     \;\Longrightarrow\;
     \sqrt{r_n}=\frac{\sqrt R\,\sqrt{r_{n-1}}}{\sqrt R-\sqrt{r_{n-1}}}\,.
   \]
   If we set \(s_n=\sqrt{r_n}\) and \(c=\sqrt R\), this recurrence is
   \[
     s_n=\frac{c\,s_{n-1}}{\,c-s_{n-1}\,}, 
     \quad s_1=1.
   \]
   One checks by induction that
   \[
     s_n=\frac{c}{\,c-(n-1)\,}\,,
     \]
   so
   \[
     r_n=s_n^2
        =\frac{R}{\bigl(c-(n-1)\bigr)^2}
        =\frac{2008}{\bigl(\sqrt{2008}-(n-1)\bigr)^2}\,.
   \]

   \[
     \sqrt{2008}-(n-1)>0
     \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
     n-1<\sqrt{2008}\approx44.82
     \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
     n<45.82.
   \]
   Hence the largest integer \(n\) is 
   \[
     n_{\max}=45.
   \]

Answer: \(\boxed{45}\).
